name: TaskLens

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixes & compliance

- **CSS `!important` cleanup.** Removed ~33 redundant `!important` declarations from `styles.css`. Selectors already win by specificity or source order; the seven remaining instances (Obsidian chrome `display: none` overrides) are intentional and unchanged.
- **Duplicate padding rule removed.** A second `.tasklens-dashboard-content { padding: 12px }` block that conflicted with the first was eliminated; the canonical value is now `12px` in a single rule.
- **License metadata corrected.** `package.json` now uses the proper SPDX identifier `PolyForm-Noncommercial-1.0.0`.
- **Release notes.** The GitHub release workflow now extracts the relevant CHANGELOG section and includes it as the release body.
- **README typo fixed.** "by each widget to your liking" → "by customizing each widget to your liking".

---

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## TaskLens 1.3.0

### What's new

**Kanban board view** — tasks grouped into Active, Upcoming, Urgent, Overdue, and Completed columns. Drag a card between columns to write the date change back to your Markdown file. Enable via **Settings → Show board view**.

**Inline edit & delete** — hover any task in the list to reveal edit (pencil) and delete (trash) buttons. Edit opens Quick Add pre-filled with existing data. Enable via **Settings → Appearance → Show task action buttons**.

**Start date & recurrence dropdown in Quick Add** — Quick Add now includes a start date picker and a structured recurrence dropdown (daily / weekly / biweekly / monthly / quarterly / yearly) replacing the old free-text field.

**Task notes in list view** — tasks with `[notes:: ...]` metadata now display their notes inline below the task row.

**7-day velocity histogram & topic urgency** — two new stats in the Stats widget: daily completion counts for the past 7 days, and your most urgent topic by urgent-to-total ratio.

### Bug fixes

- Stats cards now respect the topic filter
- Stat box colours restored (Upcoming and Total were broken)
- Custom `start::` / `due::` key names now respected everywhere
- Date off-by-one in non-UTC timezones fixed in Quick Add
- StatsView colour theme now matches Dashboard

### Accessibility

Full pass across all components — `title` on every icon-only button, `aria-label`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, `for`/`id` on filter dropdowns, Enter key submission in Quick Add.

### Performance

Statistics and grouped task lists are now cached per render cycle. Large vaults will notice the improvement.

---

**Install / update:** via the BRAT plugin, or grab `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the assets below.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Quick bug patching and optimization of codebase

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

**Recurring task urgency restored to date-based logic.**
Recurring tasks no longer jump straight to Urgent regardless of their due date. They now follow the standard progression — Upcoming → Urgent → Overdue — based on actual proximity.

**Task edits no longer silently wipe metadata.**
Editing a task title through the UI previously reconstructed the line from scratch, dropping `[start:: ...]`, `[repeat:: ...]`, and completion stamps. The update is now surgical — only the title and due date fields are touched; everything else is preserved in place.

**StatsView state no longer lost on workspace reload.**
`StatsView.getState()` was returning only its own state and discarding Obsidian's internal leaf state. It now merges with `super.getState()` as the other views already did, so position and context survive restarts.

**Floating promise in plugin ribbon initialisation resolved.**
The `addRibbonIcon` handler in `main.ts` is now properly `async` with `await this.saveSettings()`, preventing an unhandled promise from being silently abandoned on startup.

## Performance

**Timeline layout reduced from O(N²) to O(N).**
`TimelineComponent` now builds a `dayIndexMap` once per render instead of calling `findIndex` for every visible task bar. On wide viewports with many tasks this was the dominant render cost.

**Regex allocations eliminated from the parser hot path.**
All dynamic regexes in `TaskParser.parseTaskMetadata` are now `private static readonly` class properties, compiled once at class load time. Previously they were recreated on every task line parsed during a vault scan.

## Type safety & code quality

- Collapsed `?: T | null` double-optionality on `Task.completionDate` and `Task.recurrence` to standard `?: T`
- Applied `private readonly` to constructor-injected dependencies across service and view layers
- Added explicit `void` return type annotations to all rendering and lifecycle methods
- Removed commented-out dead code (`taskMatchesStatus`, `setSortBy`, `TaskSortBy`)
- Added `null` guard before `as` cast in `StatsView.setState()` and `TaskListView.setState()`

## Infrastructure

- CI pipeline now runs the full Vitest suite as a mandatory gate before build — a clean build with failing tests no longer passes
- All workflows updated to `npm ci`, with Node/npm dependency caching
- Release workflow trigger corrected from `v*` to `[0-9]*` to match the project's unprefixed versioning policy
- `verify-build` scripts resolve `npm` paths correctly on all Windows environments
- GitHub Actions dependencies bumped: `actions/checkout` → v6, `actions/setup-node` → v6 (Node 24), `codeql-action` → v4
- Dependabot configured for both npm and GitHub Actions dependencies

releases.author: nightaqua
releases.description:
**What’s fixed**

* Re-completing tasks now works even if they already contain older completion markers (`completion::`, Tasks-plugin ✅, or `yyyy-mm-dd` stamps).
* Timeline filtering in the dashboard is corrected; the **Show** dropdown now consistently filters both the task list and timeline.
* Fixed timezone-related off-by-one issues by parsing dates at local midnight.

**Note**

* Storage format remains `yyyy-mm-dd`, while display and completion timestamps use `dd-mm-yyyy` (with `HH:mm` for completion) [might require fixing this logic later #todo].

releases.author: nightaqua
releases.description:
### What's fixed
- Checking a task in the editor no longer flickers back to unchecked
- Unchecking a completed task now correctly removes its completion stamp, so re-checking it works properly
- Recurring task automation now works even when TaskLens views are closed (e.g. focus mode)
- Monthly and yearly recurrence no longer overshoots: January 31 + 1 month gives February 28, not March 3
- The Upcoming stats card is back (it was silently missing)
- Settings no longer trigger a full vault rescan on every change

### What's new
- Recurring tasks are collapsed into a single row in the list view and a single bar on the timeline: no more duplicates flooding your views. A - ×N badge shows how many cycles you've completed
- TaskLens now coexists cleanly with the Tasks plugin and Dataview! It reads their completion and recurrence markers before writing, so you won't get double-stamped metadata
- The "Completed" stat can be toggled between all-time and today independently on each dashboard widget. It's no longer a single global setting
- Settings accordion sections remember whether they were open or closed
- Dates now display as dd-mm-yyyy as per writing of the plugin

### Compatibility note
Tasks written with [due:: ...], [start:: ...], or the 📅 emoji are fully supported. Recurrence written with the Tasks plugin's 🔁 emoji is recognised for display. TaskLens will write clones using its own [repeat:: ...] format.
